title: Level 210
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 210: "Mountain Slide"}}
[[File:MountainSlideSDIcon.png|thumb|170px]]
'''Threat Index: Class 5'''
'''》Exceedingly Unsafe'''
'''》Unstable'''
'''》Presence of Lethal Entity'''
{{Background|file=MountainSlide.jpg}}
{{Blurimage|file=MountainSlide.jpg|caption=An image of Level 210, taken a substantial way into the level.|hovertext=Slide}} ==Description== '''Level 210''', also known as 'Mountain Slide', resembles a large, snowy mountain, with wanderers always appearing at a small flat area on one side near the peak of the mountain. Except for a few minor exceptions that will be explained later, the mountain is completely covered in snow. The temperature of the level remains at a cold -10°C (14°F). The wanderer who ends up in '''Level 210''' receives one or more of the following childhood toys: a sleigh, sled, snowboard, and a pair of skis with ski poles. The beginning point evokes nostalgia and prompts the individual to recall previous encounters with the stated toys. These items only appear if the individual had them in their childhood and seem identical to the ones a wanderer has previously used, with the only alteration being the size to fit the wanderer. If said wanderer did not have any of these objects in their previous life, they are given a sled with a steering wheel. Moreover, an unknown anomaly will ensure that any sleighs or sleds used will not be more liable to tip over than other sliding equipment despite that being the case commonly. Furthermore, if a person possessed a damaged sleigh/snowboard/skis in their childhood, an unknown anomaly will ensure it still functions properly. If it was significantly damaged (for example, huge portions missing), the item gets replaced with a repaired replica. After 30 seconds of being in the level, a snowstorm will begin to quickly form via unknown means at the peak of the mountain, quickly gaining mass and turning into a living avalanche with glowing light blue eyes, so one must be as quick as possible to start their slide down the peak to avoid immediately getting caught in the Avalanche and possibly even get well ahead of it. The wanderer's goal in '''Level 210''' is to get to the bottom of the mountain without getting caught by the Avalanche for 50 miles (80,46 km), which chases the unlucky wanderer who ended up here. On their way to the exit, they encounter obstacles like small cliffs, destroyed shacks, and trees. The shacks always appear degenerated, with some shacks being more damaged and visibly unstable than others. Most of them are almost empty of furniture, with some being devoid of furniture whatsoever. Any furniture present, which is as languished as the shacks they occupy, is always placed to the sides of the shacks, respective to the wanderer's path and leaving the walls as the only major obstacle that the shacks present. The hindrances and their positions randomize every time someone ends up in this environment. Wanderers reported that unique obstacles, like forests, destroyed buildings, and deep gaps that can cause a wanderer to trip over the cracks appear on the trail. Witnesses also stated that they encountered frozen hospital hallways inside mountain tunnels coated in ice, which contained different elements. These elements ranged from windows lighting the path (even though, as previously stated, the hallway is inside a mountain tunnel), inaccessible doors lining the hallways, to the walls and the ceiling being overlaid in ice, albeit they looked fine. There are instances where there are no windows or doors, and the walls and ceiling are not overlaid in ice. Besides that, the hallway is damaged, emits blue lights, and possesses a combination of the mentioned attributes. On the sides of the snowy path, thick forests neatly border the course, preventing travelers from going off the pathway. At multiple points of the track, walls made out of the snow-destroyed buildings and typical barriers one finds on a ski path also border the sides, while sometimes there are no barriers present in the first place. The parts of the mountain not covered in snow are the ruined huts, the undersides of the trees, portions of some of the cliff faces present in the level, and sometimes the walls and the ceiling of the hospital hallways. ==Entities== There is only one entity known to reside in '''Level 210''', that being The Avalanche. Due to the ever-present dangers and the overall layout of the level, the latter of which likely would have allowed visitors to spot any other entities present, there are likely no other entities on the level whatsoever. ===The Avalanche=== The Avalanche is an extremely powerful and seemingly unstoppable entity in '''Level 210''', having the form of a raging snow avalanche with two glowing light-blue eyes that distinguish it as an entity. As was earlier mentioned, once a wanderer has spent 30 seconds within '''Level 210''', The Avalanche will begin to manifest, starting as a whirling snowstorm and then quickly gaining mass to turn into an avalanche, after which its eyes will open and it will immediately start chasing the wanderer who ended up in the level. Once it has started the chase, The Avalanche will never stop in its pursuit until the wanderer in question escapes the level. It is widely believed that The Avalanche only manifests when a wanderer remains in '''Level 210''' for 30 seconds, as the entity has never been reported to have already manifested before a wanderer's arrival in the level or before the first 30 seconds have passed. Some have speculated that The Avalanche is malevolent and does not simply kill out of necessity but out of choice, reasoning that The Avalanche only appears when a wanderer has been in '''Level 210''' for 30 seconds — if that is indeed the case — because it lives to cause pain and death and does not take any interest in any other activities whatsoever. It is unknown what state The Avalanche exists in when it has not yet manifested or whether it is even alive or sentient during these periods. Theories on what state The Avalanche might be in during the aforementioned periods of non-manifestation include but are not limited to the entity existing in a non-corporeal form, existing as living snow that has not yet started to transition into an avalanche, and not existing at all. The Avalanche can plow through and destroy most obstacles in its path, except for the mountain itself and the tunnels. Often, The Avalanche will change shape to fit through the tunnels and continue pursuing the wanderer it is chasing, making it possible for the tunnels to collapse to slow it down, though it will eventually either burst free and continue its pursuit or go back the way it came and continue said pursuit on the surface. Occasionally, however, it will not follow the wanderer into the tunnel and instead trail them on the surface, sometimes catching them at the end of said tunnel or the very least surprising them. {{Collapse|num=1|initial=
'''However, once one passes the 25-mile mark...'''
|final=
'''However, once one passes the 25-mile mark...'''
|body=
'''...it only gets more dangerous from there.'''
The ground begins to shake as soon as individuals reach the second half of the course, and the environment changes dramatically. After that, the mountain one is on reveals itself as a volcano; the sky changes to red but gradually darkens and dims the wanderer's vision with each mile passed, alongside the sun, and the clouds turn pitch black. The volcano erupts and transforms the Avalanche into a lava version of itself with red eyes instead of blue, though the Avalanche still retains the same sentience and behavior as before. The volcano shakes violently every five miles, causing new cracks to form and shooting up massive jets of lava that land and puddle on the track, melting the ice. Rocks roll down the path behind the wanderer due to the constant shaking, damaging the obstacles behind and in front of the unfortunate person. The route's angle gradually increases with each mile passed, making it harder to avoid obstructions. The gaps enlarge dramatically, reaching a width of five meters. Falling into the mentioned gaps will lead to [[The Void]]. The number of obstacles quadruples and most hindrances begin to burn without getting damaged. The snow is very heat resistant, only melting if the distance between the Lava Avalanche and the snow decreases to 5 meters or less. The trees' height rises to 30 meters, forcing wanderers to increase their distance between the Lava Avalanche to prevent being crushed by one of the knocked-down trees. One will frequently find forks that split into two tunnels, one of which is always a red and burning hospital hallway with a blaring siren and a wall made of fire at the end. The wall made of fire causes the environment to warp into [[Level 666]] upon passing through it; otherwise, it will burn the wanderer to ash as they do so. The other underpass consists of a tunnel made of volcanic rocks riddled with massive cracks. Lava frequently drips from the cracks and forms puddles on the floor. Avoiding collisions with the walls is crucial, as colliding with them causes the tunnel to collapse. The red-misty volcanic tunnels are frequently so unstable that they collapse on their own after a wanderer passes through them, obstructing the route behind them and decelerating the Lava Avalanche. The only visible light sources at five miles are the Lava Avalanche and the red outlines that cover the mentioned obstacles. Because of the darkness and low brightness emitted by the contours, a powerful flashlight is required to avoid colliding with any obstacles. Dust from the erupted volcano accumulates on the track and can easily blind or damage the eye. Humiliating voices of beloved ones confront the wanderer with sentences like "Give up, you'll never make it" or "Just die already!" Tumbling rocks double in number and size, and their outlines are noticeably brighter than other obstacles. If the unfortunate wanderer makes it to the final mile, they will come across a massive, never-ending crater beneath the track, complete with a massive steel ramp with red glowing outlines on the course's edge. The appearance of said ramp is slightly damaged and partially molten by the lava, resulting in victims injuring themselves on the ramp or falling down the crater. If the ramp becomes damaged enough, it will quickly repair itself to its semi-damaged state before a wanderer reaches it. When Level 210 originally enters its lava state, a layer of ice beneath the trail and above the pit shatters and drops into the crater. The ski track and barriers hover above the hole due to an unknown anomalous force released from within it. The aforementioned voices will now attempt to persuade the individual to escape the volcano and the Lava Avalanche, whispering phrases like "You can do it" and "Don't give up." Upon jumping off the ramp, the floating trail crashes down and falls into the pit, and the sky quickly returns to normal, with the outlines and the black fog gone. Getting to the opposite side of the ravine causes one to clip through the snowy ground, sending one to a random level that consists of or includes a prominent mountain, guaranteeing the wanderer never winds up in Level 210 again. Failure to do so results in the sky returning to the mentioned red sky and the voices resuming their harsh negative comments and depressing the wanderer, draining the person's sanity. Once the wanderer's sanity is completely depleted and provided the wanderer does not die from slamming into the inside of the ravine, the environment transitions to [[The Void]].
==Colonies & Outposts== Due to the constant dangers, creating outposts is infeasible. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== *Any wanderer has a chance of entering this level randomly from any cold or snow levels, such as [[Level 44]], [[Level 125]], or [[Level 202]]. *Finding and going near a pair of unknown skis in [[Level 193]] will teleport one here. ===Exits=== *As mentioned above, passing the pit causes one to noclip through the ground, sending one to a random Level that contains a mountain. *As mentioned above, falling into the gaps and in the pit in the Lava section brings one to [[The Void]]. *Passing through the wall of fire at the end of the hospital hallway in the Lava section has a chance to transport one back to [[Level 666]]. *No clipping while sliding has a chance to transport one to [[Level 415]]
[[Level 210/A Snowy Rebirth|'''All is not what it seems...''']]
